com.hp.workpath.api.printer

Class PrintAttributesCaps

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.printer.PrintAttributesCaps

  • public class PrintAttributesCaps
extends java.lang.Object
    Sets of attributes provided by the device when requesting a print.
    Since:
    API 1

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.AutoFit> getAutoFitList()
      Retrieves if AutoFit option is supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.CollateMode> getCollateModeList()
      Retrieves collate options supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.ColorMode> getColorModeList()
      Retrieves the groups of color sets.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.DocumentFormat> getDocumentFormatList()
      Retrieves document formats supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Duplex> getDuplexList()
      Retrieves duplex options supported by the device
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Finishings> getFinishingsList()
      Retrieves finishings options supported on the device.
      int getMaxCopies()
      Retrieves max number of copies a user can provide
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Orientation> getOrientationList()
      Retrieves orientation options supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.OutputBin> getOutputBinList()
      Retrieves output-bin options supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PaperSize> getPaperSizeList()
      Retrieves paper sizes supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PaperSource> getPaperSourceList()
      Retrieves paper sources supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PaperType> getPaperTypeList()
      Retrieves paper types supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PrintQuality> getPrintQualityList()
      Retrieves print-quality options supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Source> getSourceList()
      Retrieves file sources supported on the device.
      java.util.List<PrintAttributes.StapleMode> getStapleModeList()
      Retrieves staple options supported on the device.

    • Method Detail

      • getColorModeList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.ColorMode> getColorModeList()
        Retrieves the groups of color sets.
        Returns:
        the color modes supported.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the ColorMode from PrintAttributes.ColorMode
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getDuplexList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Duplex> getDuplexList()
        Retrieves duplex options supported by the device
        Returns:
        List of duplex options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the Duplex from PrintAttributes.Duplex
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getMaxCopies

        public int getMaxCopies()
        Retrieves max number of copies a user can provide
        Returns:
        maximum number of copies.

        • maximum value of copies should be 32000 and it shouldn't accept value above 32000

        Since:
        API 1

      • getAutoFitList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.AutoFit> getAutoFitList()
        Retrieves if AutoFit option is supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of AutoFit options for printer.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the AutoFit from PrintAttributes.AutoFit
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getStapleModeList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.StapleMode> getStapleModeList()
        Retrieves staple options supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of Staple options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the StapleMode from PrintAttributes.StapleMode
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getPaperSourceList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PaperSource> getPaperSourceList()
        Retrieves paper sources supported on the device.
        Returns:

        List of paper sources from PrintAttributes.PaperSource.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the PaperSource from PrintAttributes.PaperSource
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getPaperSizeList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PaperSize> getPaperSizeList()
        Retrieves paper sizes supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of paper sizes.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the PaperSize from PrintAttributes.PaperSize
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getPaperTypeList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PaperType> getPaperTypeList()
        Retrieves paper types supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of paper types.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the PaperType from PrintAttributes.PaperType
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getDocumentFormatList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.DocumentFormat> getDocumentFormatList()
        Retrieves document formats supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of document formats.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the DocumentFormat from PrintAttributes.DocumentFormat
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getCollateModeList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.CollateMode> getCollateModeList()
        Retrieves collate options supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of Collate options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the CollateMode from PrintAttributes.CollateMode
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1

      • getOrientationList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Orientation> getOrientationList()
        Retrieves orientation options supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of Orientation options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the Orientation from PrintAttributes.Orientation
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 5

      • getPrintQualityList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.PrintQuality> getPrintQualityList()
        Retrieves print-quality options supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of Print Quality options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the PrintQuality from PrintAttributes.PrintQuality
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 5

      • getOutputBinList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.OutputBin> getOutputBinList()
        Retrieves output-bin options supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of Output-bin options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the OutputBin from PrintAttributes.OutputBin
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 5

      • getFinishingsList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Finishings> getFinishingsList()
        Retrieves finishings options supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of Finishings options.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the Finishings from PrintAttributes.Finishings
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 5

      • getSourceList

        public java.util.List<PrintAttributes.Source> getSourceList()
        Retrieves file sources supported on the device.
        Returns:
        List of supported sources.

        • Return can't be empty and should be either any of the Source from PrintAttributes.Source
        • An empty list indicates that the option is not supported by the printer.

        Since:
        API 1
